folio.label: 162r
folio.l:
wounded , we shuln do so ententyf besinesse , fro day to nygút
that witú the gce of god , she shal be hool & sound , as sone as
is possible

¢ Almost rigút in the same wyse the Phisiciens
answerden , saue that they seiden a fewe wordes more ,

that
rigút as maladies ben cured , by here contries , rigút so shal
man warissúe werre , by vengeau~ce

¢ hise Neyghbores ful of En-
vie , hise feyned frendes , that semed reconsiled , hise flatereres

maden sembant of wepyng , and empeired and aggregged mechel
of this matere , in preisyng gretly Melibee , of mygút of power
of richesse and of frendes , despisyng the power of hise aduäsaries

and seiden outrely , that he a noon , shulde wreke hi~ on his foos ,
and bigynne werre

¢ Vp ros tha~ne , an aduocat that was wys
by leue and by counseil , of othere that were wyse , And seide ,

lor-
dynges the nede , for the whicú we ben assembled in this place ,
is ful heuy thyng~ö , & an heigú matere

by cause of the wrong
and of the wykkednesse that hatú be doon , and eke by resou~ of
the grete damages , that in tyme comynge ben possible to
fallen , for the same cause ,

and eke biresou~ of the gret richesse
and power of the ¹ties bothe ,

for the whicú resou~s , it were a
ful gret ¹ile to erren in this matere ,

wherfore Melibeus , æis
is oure sentence , we cou~seille ©ow a bouen alle thyngö , that ri©t
a noon æè do thy diligence in kepyngö of thy ¸pre ¹sone , i~ swicú
a wyse , that æè ne wante noon espie ne wacche , thy body for
to saue

¢ And after that we cou~seille , that in thyn hous thow
sette suffisau~t garnysou~ , so that they moun , as wel thy body
as thyn hous defende

¢ But certes for to meeue werre , ne so-
deynly for to doon vengeaunce , we moun nat deme in so litel
tyme , that it were ¸fitable ,

wherfore we axen leiser and espace
to haue deliberaciou~ , in this cas to deme ,

for the comune ¸uäbe
seitú this , he that sone demetú ; Soone shal repente ,

And eke
men seyn , that thilke Iuge is wys , that sone vnderstondetú
a matere , and Iuggetú by leyser ,

for al be it so , that al tariy~g
be a noyful , algates it is nat to repreeue in ©euy~g of Iugeme~t
ne in vengeau~ce takyng , whan it is suffisau~t and resonable ,

And
that shewed oure lord Iúu cést by ensample , for whan that the wo~-
man that was taken in aduoutrie , was brougút in his päsence
to knowen what shulde be don witú hire ¹sone , al be it , that he
wist wel hi~ self , what that he wolde answere , ©et ne wolde he
nat answere sodeynly , but he wolde haue deliberaciou~ , And in
the ground he wroot twies ,

and by theise causes we axen deli-
beraciou~ , and we shuln tha~ne by the grace of god , cou~seille the
thyngö , that shal be ¸fitable

¢ Vp stirte tha~ne , the ©onge folk
atones , and the most ¹tie of that compaignye , han skorned æeise
